AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 6 MILES NW OF CADIZ ALONG HWY 274 AND LIFTED 4 MILES NW OF CADIZ. THE DURATION OF THE EVENT WAS FROM 740 TO 745 PM. AT LEAST A DOZEN TREES WERE UPROOTED OR BROKEN WITH ONE POWER LINE PARTIALLY DOWNED BY A FALLEN TREE. SEVERAL PIECES OF METAL ROOFING FROM A BARN WERE LOFTED INTO A NEARBY TREE. PATH LENGTH WAS 2 MILES WITH PEAK WINDS OF 80 MPH.
NEAR HIGHWAY 51 NORTH AND HIGHWAY NORTHEAST OF LITHIUM.

*** 1 FATAL, 12 INJ *** EF-4 TORNADO WITH A 50.4 MILE PATH THAT ENDED 1.8 MILES SW OF CHRISTOPHER, IL. PEAK WINDS WERE 180 MPH. SOME OF THE WORST DAMAGE WAS ON NORTHWEST SIDE OF PERRYVILLE WITH 5 HOUSES LEVELED. PEAK WIDTH WAS 0.6 MILES. DAMAGE PATH SHOWED MULTIPLE VORTEX CHARACTERISTICS WITH GROUND STRIATIONS/SCARRING TO BARE GROUND IN PERRY COUNTY NEAR MISSISSIPPI RIVER. TORNADO WAS ON GROUND FOR 1 HOUR AND 2 MINUTES LIFTING AT 857PM.

EF-4 TORNADO WITH A 50.4 MILE PATH THAT ENDED 1.8 MILES SW OF CHRISTOPHER, IL. PEAK WINDS WERE 180 MPH. SOME OF THE WORST DAMAGE WAS ON NORTHWEST SIDE OF PERRYVILLE WITH 5 HOUSES LEVELED. PEAK WIDTH WAS 0.6 MILES. DAMAGE PATH SHOWED MULTIPLE VORTEX CHARACTERISTICS WITH GROUND STRIATIONS/SCARRING TO BARE GROUND IN PERRY COUNTY NEAR MISSISSIPPI RIVER. TORNADO WAS ON GROUND FOR 1 HOUR AND 2 MINUTES LIFTING AT 857PM.

EF-0 TORNADO NEAR CARBONDALE, IL. PEAK WIND 75 MPH. PATH LENGTH 4.1 MILES. MAX WIDTH 25 YRDS. BEGAN 8.4 MILES WSW OF CARBONDALE IL AND ENDED 4.5 MILES WEST OF COMMUNITY. THE WEAK TORNADO SKIPPED ALONG THIS PATH PRODUCING MAINLY TREE DAMAGE CONSISTING OF BROKEN TREE BRANCHES. WIDESPREAD HAIL REPORTS WERE RECEIVED AROUND THE SAME TIME.

EF0 TORNADO. MAX WIND SPEED 70 MPH. PATH FROM 4 MILES SOUTH OF LYNNVILLE IN GRAVES COUNTY TO JUST EAST OF LYNN GROVE IN CALLOWAY COUNTY. PATH LENGTH 8.4 MILES, MAX WIDTH 30 YARDS. MINOR DAMAGE TO BARN ROOFS, HUNDREDS OF TREES SNAPPED HALF WAY UP, SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED.

EF-1 TORNADO NEAR CARTERVILLE, IL. MAX WIND 95 MPH WITH PATH LENGTH 7.5 MILES. MAX WIDTH 25 YRDS. BEGAN 2.2 MILES SE OF TOWN AND ENDED 3.5 MILES NORTH OF MARION, IL. THE WEAK TORNADO SKIPPED ALONG THIS PATH PRODUCING MAINLY TREE DAMAGE CONSISTING OF BROKEN TREE BRANCHES WITH A FEW UPROOTED TREES.
POWER POLE ON BUILDING. BLEW PART OF METAL ROOF OFF AND SHINGLES ALONG WITH THE BACK WALL OUT OF AN OUTBUILDING AND BUCKLED ANOTHER WALL.

EF1 TORNADO FROM 5 NE OF LINTON TO 3 SW OF CALEDONIA. 8 MILE TRACK, 400 YARD MAXIMUM WIDTH, PEAK WIND 105 MPH. HUNDREDS OF TREES SNAPPED/UPROOTED. 1 GRAIN BIN DESTROYED. MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO NUMEROUS BARNS. SIDING/ROOF DAMAGE TO MANY HOMES.

EF1 TORNADO WITH 100 MPH WINDS WENT 15.3 MILES ACROSS NEW MADRID AND MISSISSIPPI COUNTIES. PEAK WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS. TORNADO LIFTED 3 MILES NNE OF ANNISTON AT 452 AM. DOZENS OF TREES EITHER UPROOTED OR HAD LARGE BRANCHES BROKEN OFF. NUMEROUS POWER POLES SNAPPED OR BLOWN OVER. DOZENS OF IRRIGATION SYSTEMS WERE BLOWN OVER.
SEVERAL UTILITY POLES SNAPPED ALONG HIGHWAY AA FROM 2 MILES NORTH OF HIGHWAY 80 NORTH TO THE COUNTY LINE. A COUPLE OF IRRIGATION PIVOTS FLIPPED.
PORTION OF ROOF OFF OF A HOUSE ON STATE HIGHWAY AA. EXTENSIVE SIDING AND SHINGLE DAMAGE AND A CARPORT BLOWN AWAY AT SAME RESIDENCE.

EF-1 TORNADO. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 105 MPH. PATH LENGTH ABOUT 6 MILES. PATH WIDTH 100 YARDS. MUCH OF PATH WAS IN REMOTE AREA OF MARK TWAIN NATIONAL FOREST. NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED WHERE THE TORNADO CROSSED U.S. HIGHWAY 67 NORTH OF THE U.S. 60 SPLIT.

EF-2 TORNADO TRAVELLED FROM APPROXIMATELY 6 MILES NORTH OF DONIPHAN MO TO 12 MILES NORTHEAST OF DONIPHAN MO. WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 120 MPH. HUNDREDS TO THOUSANDS OF LARGE HARDWOOD AND PINE TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. ONE BARN/SHED DESTROYED. DAMAGE WAS MOSTLY RURAL.

EF-1 TORNADO. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED 95 MPH. PATH LENGTH 2.2 MILES. PATH WIDTH 75 YARDS. MOST INTENSE DAMAGE ON HIGHWAY 51, WHERE A RESIDENCE LOST DOORS AND WINDOWS. NEARBY LARGE BARN LOST A WALL AND PORTION OF METAL ROOF. ROOFING DEBRIS DEPOSITED ABOUT ONE MILE TO THE EAST-SOUTHEAST. GAZEBO BLOWN 50 TO 100 YARDS.

EF1 TORNADO. MAX WIND SPEED 95 MPH. PATH FROM SOUTH SIDE OF MURRAY AT HIGH SCHOOL TO 5 SOUTHEAST OF MURRAY ON HIGHWAY 121. PATH LENGTH 4.8 MILES, MAX WIDTH 145 YARDS. DUGOUT ROOFS AT SCHOOL BLOWN INTO HOME AND CAR ACROSS THE STREET. TREES UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG PATH. AT END OF PATH A LARGE PORTION OF ROOF AND SOME TRUSSES BLOWN OFF WELL BUILT METAL BUILDING. PLASTIC DRIVEN INTO SIDE OF A GARAGE. BUILDING DESTROYED BY FALLING TREES.

EF1 TORNADO BEGAN 4.6 MI SE OF CLINTON KY AND TRAVELED 12.5 MILES AND LIFTED 3.2 MI NNE OF WINGO KY. 150 YDS MAX PATH WIDTH. PEAK WIND SPEED 95 MPH. 3 DOZEN HOMES/BUILDINGS HAD MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE. LARGE CHICKEN HOUSE LOST 1/4 OF ITS ROOF. SEVERAL SMALL OUTBUILDINGS/GARAGES WERE DESTROYED. HUNDREDS OF TREES/TREE LIMBS BLOWN DOWN. 4 EYEWITNESSES SAW THE TORNADO NEAR FULGHAM, WHERE THE MOST OF THE CONCENTRATED DAMAGE WAS.

A SECTION OF HIGHWAY 45 WILL BE CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER OVER THE ROADWAY.
THE EAST BOUND LANE OF ROUTE 13 IS CLOSED AT WALL STREET DUE TO HIGH WATER.
STEWART STREET IN THE CITY OF CARMI WILL BE CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER. SEVERAL OTHER STREETS WILL HAVE SIGNS PUT UP AS WATER RUNS OVER THE ROAD.
A CREEK THAT IS OUT OF ITS BANKS IS FLOWING OVER THE ROADWAY.
NUMEROUS ROADS ON NW SIDE OF HERRIN COMPLETELY FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE. WATER RESCUES HAVE BEEN EXECUTED IN TOWN.
7.93 INCHES OF RAIN IN 11 HOURS. EVERY POND AND CREEK ON HIS PROPERTY IS SO FILLED IT HAS BECOME ONE LARGE LAKE. WATER IS POURING OFF HILLY FARM FILLED MAKING DRIVING ON SOME COUNTY ROADS IMPOSSIBLE.
CLOSING ROAD TO THE DEVILS KITCHEN SPILLWAY. ALSO, IN THE CITY OF MARION AND HERRIN, THERE ARE NUMEROUS ROADS WITH WATER OVER THE ROADWAY.

SEVERAL LOCATIONS ON NORTH ST. JOSEPH ROAD FROM DARMSTADT TO THE NORTHERN EVANSVILLE CITY LIMITS IS UNDER WATER, WITH SEVERAL INTERSECTIONS UNDER WATER AS WELL. WATER IN HOME AT THE INTERSECTION OF ST. JOSEPH ROAD AND MILL ROAD.
NUMEROUS ROADS UNDERWATER. ESTIMATED RAINFALL APPROXIMATELY 6 INCHES.
STATE HIGHWAY 61 CLOSED BETWEEN STATE ROUTE 64 AND SPURGEON. NUMEROUS COUNTY ROADS ARE IMPASSABLE.
INTERSECTION OF GOODALL AND JOHNSON STREET UNDER 1/2 FOOT OF WATER. MAIN CREEK GOING THROUGH TOWN IS NEARING BANKFUL.
FLOODING OF CREEKS REPORTED THROUGHOUT CARTER COUNTY. EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT IS EVACUATING RESIDENTS IN PART OF FREMONT.

HWY K IN SOUTHEAST PART OF COUNTY CLOSED NORTH OF HWY B DUE TO WATER OVER THE ROADWAY.
NUMEROUS ROADS IN THE COUNTY CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER INCLUDING HIGHWAY 49 SOUTH OF MILL SPRING. OTHER ROADS INCLUDE SECTIONS OF HIGHWAYS 143, 34, FF, KK, A, AND C.
NUMEROUS COUNTY ROADS IN SOUTHERN PART OF COUNTY ARE IMPASSABLE DUE TO HIGH WATER INCLUDING A PORTION OF HWY 160.
HWY 160 EAST AT BUTLER COUNTY LINE AND ALSO IN FAIRDEALING HAS WATER OVER THE ROADWAY. COUNTY RD JJ HAS WATER OVER ROAD.

EF-2 TORNADO. PEAK WINDS 125 MPH. PATH LENGTH 4 MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 150 YARDS. TORNADO BEGAN 1/2 MILE NORTH OF MATTHEWS AND LIFTED 1 MILE SOUTH OF SIKESTON. ROOF REMOVED FROM ONE HOUSE AND PART OF ONE EXTERIOR WALL WAS BLOWN INWARD. ONE BARN LEVELED. TWO OTHER BARNS DAMAGED. SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED.
